description: You are a calculus professor creating 3D course materials for a Calculus II unit on solids of revolution. In GeoGebra, create an interactive visualization demonstrating the solid formed by rotating the curve f(x) = sqrt(x) over the interval [0, 4] around the x-axis. The visualization must include the original curve, the surface of revolution, a slider controlling a moveable cross-sectional plane at position x = a showing the circular cross-section at that x-value, and a text display showing the computed volume from 0 to the slider value a using the disk method formula. Save the file as 'solid_revolution.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/
